reviews.review_description: Absolutely disappointing. Nothing about this feels realistic. I had high hopes, and was hoping it could be a little useful, but I think if anything, this would make me worse. I really hope there are some major improvements that make this useful. It's a great idea in theory.

developer_response.body: HI.  We just uploaded a new version.  Give it another try.  I think you'll find it more playable.  We will continue to upgrade and improve.  Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_title: physics needed to be greatly improved
reviews.review_description: I agree with hypheneater: other vr apps for tennis or ping pong are much better simulations re ball weight, bounciness, amount of force needed to move the ball.  it's hard to hit and aim (even on easy)  and it's hurting my shoulder. Dev replied but communication is difficult (or not working). Since the app still does not work well I will ask for a refund and hopefully purchase in the future if/when it works. P.S. I don't believe the positive reviews and I,m changing my rating to 1 star. Hopefully this will get the devs to get to work on the repair.

developer_response.body: Hi and thanks for taking the time to write.  Question to help us:  do you feel you are needing to apply too much force to move the ball?  You feel it needs more spring off the paddle and the court?  Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts.  We hope to improve this with time.

reviews.review_description: I am really rooting for this app to improve.  It fills a void and many real-life PBaller's would enjoy a VR app with authentic drills to improve our game.  In it's current state, this is not that app.  The concept is on-target and it contains the right variety of drills.  It's failure is with very poor ball physics.  As result, it is terribly frustrating to play and I can't see continuing to invest my time in the game until developer releases an update that improves the game play.  There are currently two other PB games in the development lab that are much further along in development and have included drills in addition to the main gameplay.   Both developers of those apps are remarkably responsive to user input and releasing updates frequently.  Money is better spent supporting those two apps unless we see a big improvement in this game.  Would love for the developer to prove me wrong and release a major new update that improves ball physics.

developer_response.body: I have circle back to this.  If you still have it, please give it another try.  I just uploaded a new build.  Practice wall is much more playable with an updated game logic (try to hit the red moving target).  The longer games (Return of Serve) will be more playable, I believe.  Thank you for getting the app, and I'm very sorry for the long delay.

reviews.review_description: Don't download this if you play Pickleball. Not accurate at all.
The physics are too far off too be useful. Have to swing the paddle way to hard to get the ball over the net when dinking.

developer_response.body: Sorry for the crazy delay.  If you still have the app, give it another try.  I improved the ball physics and significantly improved Practice Wall.  The goal is to hit the red moving target.  More drills coming.  I'll upload them as soon as possible as a free add on to this app.  Thank you again and I apologize for the delay.

reviews.review_description:
This is not ready for prime time.  The response time is off, it hits the balls out of the court.  It is in no way a good “training” tool.  I was also led to believe this was more than a training sim, and would be able to play others.

It’s not even good for training.
Please tell me how to get a refund

developer_response.body:
Sorry you are disappointed.  A few items since you address them: 

1.  Oculus App Lab exists for apps that are in development.  Oculus posts a message and requires you to acknowledge this fact.  

2.  The description of our app specifically states that ours is NOT a simulation that will enable you to play a pickleball match.  If you were "lead to believe" this, it was not by us.  This is posted and viewable before any purchase.

3.  Oculus makes refunding available through your account. We would direct you to your phone app.

We wish you the best of luck in your pickleball.

reviews.review_description: Great potential as a trainer! Looks good. Plays poorly. Paddle controls needs a lot of work. If you could approach the mechanics of “Eleven” you would be on your way to get the Pickleball community on board as a rainy day training aid. A PB singles game against AI should be easy to develop and add on. Doubles with AI and friends, now that is a different story. If you are looking for beta testers I’m all in.

developer_response.body: Hi.  Thanks for your feedback.  I just uploaded a new version that I believe will enhance playability for everyone.  I hope you give it a try.  One additional feature added is the ability to play with a 75% paddle.  Press "A" on your controller to make the paddle smaller, and press "B" to return to normal size.  Please await further changes and features coming your way.  Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_title: Super Fun and Helped My Game
reviews.review_description: Really cool to be *on a court* and practicing pickleball drills while standing in my living room!  I had never stepped onto a court before, and now I am competitive among other noobs playing a friendly game.  I work up a sweat with this, too!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review!  We did try to create a way to practice drills rather than play a game of pickleball.  Being on the court with some sights and sounds of pickleball definitely makes it more fun.

reviews.review_description:
This game has potential.  I know it’s still in development, but the calibration/feeling of the paddle hitting the ball is pretty broken.  I’m sure with more work, it will get better (I hope).

**Update:  to be more specific, it seemed the paddle face randomly does not stay oriented in the direction my hand was holding it.  Glitchy was a accurate description.  Made it difficult to make contact with the ball.  I’m not sure if this is the “slipping” issue that had been previously described.  Also, I feel the ball could use a little more bounce.  Thank you.

developer_response.body: I apologize for the long wait, but we have uploaded a new build that improves ball physics and playability.  We also changed the drill Practice Wall.

reviews.review_description:
Thank you for building this --  a work in progress, but this could be great!

Here are my notes

The paddle seems like it might be a little smaller than a normal paddle?

The ball is substantially larger than a standard pickleball.  This is most noticeable in Practice Wall, when you can hold the ball on the paddle.  Also, it would be nice if the color of the ball were a bit more neon green, since this is the most common color I usually play with.

The feel of the ball is strange and not bouncy.  In Practice Wall, you can see that the ball does not "bounce" on the paddle, but can easily be caught with no recoil.  Dropping the ball onto the court also does not bounce quite as high as I'm used to, though maybe this part is imagined or specific to the courts/balls I use.

In third shot drop, it feels like I have to put a fair amount of velocity into the swing to get the ball over the net; in reality, much less is needed and it's more of a "pushing" than a "hitting".

Smaller note: the net cord runs parallel to the horizon, but standard pickleball nets have an arc from 36" at the posts to 34" in the middle.

Defend the smash seems impossible to me.  I felt like it was really difficult to figure out where the paddle was meant to be in relation to the ball, and the ball often behaved in unpredictable ways (usually with a lot less momentum than expected) when I did manage to make contact.

Overall, I've been waiting for pickleball on Oculus for a long time so I'm stoked that somebody's working on it.  I look forward to seeing the updates.  I feel like the ball physics are way more important than # of drills or game modes, and once those are figured out, I'm 100% going to figure out how to attach the controller to a real paddle for big fun.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much.  What a tremendous and constructive review.  I'll share a few notes with you: 

1.  It's cool you notice about the net arc.  Honestly we looked at that.  One of the first things.  It requires some extra processing steps to "draw and refresh" so we set it aside.  I'll look at this again, though, down the road a spell. 

2.  In terms of the ball physics, we are working on that.  Question:  On the longer games, do you feel the spring/bounce is too much?  In other words, it seems dead off the paddle on practice wall, but does it seem too springy on Return of Serve?  We can add spring to the paddle/ball pretty easily and will take a look at doing that soon.  

3.  It's interesting you feel the ball is too big.  Will take a look at that.  It's possible to specify the size exactly.....

We are actively working on improvements and modifications.  Thank you again for your review and please stay tuned for updates....

Hi!  Update on your review.  Check out the new build.  I believe it is easier to play now.  I hope you enjoy it.  Let us know.  Thanks for playing.

reviews.review_description: I like your app, but when I use defend the slam the ball sometimes just stops when it hits the court and just dies, also please tell how I can more the net because sometimes it’s right in front of me and then to low , it’s never in the same place every time.

developer_response.body: Hi.  The delay has been ridiculous.  I apologize.  I fixed this I think and fundamentally revised the ball physics on the long ball games (Return of Serve).  They are more playable I think.  I also changed "Practice Wall" to make it more doable and to give you a moving target to hit.  More drills are in development for deployment, so I hope you give the app a try and enjoy it.  Thank you again for your feedback and sorry for the delay.

reviews.review_description: I think this would be an awesome tool for pickleballers but u can’t adjust no instructions angles off

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your feedback. Sorry for the ludicrous delay.  I have refined some of the physics to make it more playable, I think.  I also improved Practice Wall.  The goal is to hit the red moving target.  I have more drills in development and will share those with you soon.  I will post them as a free add-on to this app as soon as possible.

reviews.review_description: It's not bad. Feels pretty real. It is a challenge and I am a pickleball player. I wish they could put all the drills together into a real game because that is the fun of pickleball.

developer_response.body: Hi. Thanks for playing. I just uploaded a new version that I believe will enhance playability.  We also added a feature where you can play with a 75% size paddle.  Other drills and updates are planned.  Thanks for playing!

reviews.review_description:
There were two developmental pickleball apps as of this write up, I got them both. Promptly deleted the other one…
This will be great once the ball physics are corrected. Good enough for now to work on vision and hand eye coordination , but the ball feel is completely off. Have to hit it wicked hard to move it and not realistic at all. Can’t wait for future versions !

developer_response.body: Hi, after an overly long delay, we have worked on the ball physics, the playability of all the drills especially a) Return of Serve, b) Third Shot Drop, and c) Practice Wall.  If you check it out, would love to hear your feedback.  Thank you.  We apologize for the delay.

reviews.review_description: I use cross court, quick hands and defend the smash to warmup. VR never exactly same as real game but still helped my game go from 3.0 to 3.5 so far.

developer_response.body: Thanks, PickleMT!  Our app is designed by eye doctors who are specialists in athletic performance training.  We are please to hear your game is improving.  We will keep updating and improving the app and you keep training!

reviews.review_title: Could be a great training aid.
reviews.review_description: Is there an instruction video? I can’t get the practice wall to work.  Ball seems to be inconsistent coming off the paddle. Is there a way to adjust that?

developer_response.body: Sorry for this very long delay.  I have uploaded a new version of the app that I believe will answer some of the deficiencies.  Check particularly improved playability on the long ball games (Return of Service) and improved playability on Practice Wall with a slightly refined game.  The goal in Practice Wall is to hit the moving target.  I can upload some instruction videos soon.  Thank you for trying the app, and if you have interest and still have it, give it another shot.

reviews.review_description:
Amazed on how I can practice my pickleball skill training from the comfort of my own home. Once I figured out the right of the paddle I was able to concentrate on my form as I got to do drills. 

Yes this VR Pickleball game improved my real game. As they say practice makes perfect and I can practice and get a workout whenever I want

developer_response.body:
Hi JBlaze,

Thanks a lot for taking the time to use and review the app for us.  Thrilled you like it.  We are taking everyone's reviews seriously in the hopes of making the first ever pickleball app better and better.  Again, thank you.

reviews.review_title: I really wanted to like this more
reviews.review_description: I’m having a tough time hitting the ball altogether bc the paddle doesn’t feel stable and feels glitchy. And the image of the ball isn’t stable when it gets close either. I like the drilling aspect of it but it doesn’t feel at all like the physics of the real game so far as much as I want it to.  I know it’s only in the lab but any chance that future versions may address these issues?

developer_response.body: Hi.  Long delay I know.  I just uploaded a new version that improves (I hope) the playability of the app.  In particular the long ball games like Return of Serve (where the goal is to his the ball to the proper side of the court that matches ball color) should be more playable.  I also improved Practice Wall to make it more playable.  If you still have the app give it another try.  I'm hoping to upload more drills for everyone as an add on to this app soon.  Sorry for the delay.

reviews.review_description: If you can get the bugs out of this app, it will be awesome.  Some observations.  I have to hold the hand controller a little off center to be able to connect with the ball.    Holding the controller as intended seems to set the paddle to a slight angle.  Also, the biggest fix you need to make is to allow the player to move their position using the joystick.  You are assuming a larger play area than most people have in their homes.

developer_response.body: A new build has been uploaded that addresses some of the ball physics and control issues.  We have not added player movement, but will work on that this week.
